JUDGE JACKSON MADE A DISCLOSURE THAT SHE KNOWS MR. BACON AND ALSO AS A PRESIDING JUDGE, SHE HAS CONTACT WITH THE DISTRICT ATTORNEY'S OFFICE AND THESE CONTACTS ARE STRICTLY PROFESSIONAL AND WILL NOT HAVE ANY IMPACT ON THE COURT'S ABILITY TO MAKE A DECISION. NO OBJECTIONS BY THE PARTIES. DANIEL RAY BACON APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. BORIS REZNIKOV APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL. THE TRIAL DATE OF 6/26/17 IS CONTINUED TO 10/16/17 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. ALL DISCOVERY DEADLINES, PRETRIAL AND ALL RELATED DATES WILL TRACK THE NEW TRIAL DATE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

Motion For Leave To Take Further Deposition Of Plaintiff And To Compel Deposition
Off Calendar Mar-06-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO TAKE FURTHER DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF AND TO COMPEL DEPOSITION

Appearances:
Daniel Ray Bacon of the Law Offices of Daniel Ray Bacon (415.864.0907 / bacondr99@gmail.com) for plaintiff and responding party;
Matthew K. Yan of the Office of the City Attorney (415.554.3845 / matthew.yan@sfcityatty.org) for defendant and moving party.

Counsel inform the court they have agreed on a different date for plaintiff's deposition. The court modifies its ruling as follows: Defendant City and County's motion to take further deposition of plaintiff Twanda Bailey is granted. No later than May 3, 2026 Bailey must submit to a further session of her deposition of up to four hours in length excluding any breaks that she wishes to take. The City has shown good cause for an additional session of Bailey's deposition. Neither already served written discovery nor a deposition on written questions is a satisfactory substitute for an oral deposition. The City's questions are limited to matters occurring after Bailey's prior deposition. Bailey is entitled to any reasonable accommodation, including more frequent and longer breaks than usual, that she requests due to her medical condition. Order signed. Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)
